module-4-performance-exception-report.label: Module 4: Performance Exception Report
module-4-performance-exception-report.summary: Build a grouped exception report with row filtering, HAVING thresholds, conditional KPIs, and performance labels.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Summarize Sales by Category and Status
Filter Rows and Groups
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Start the module project. In step 1 of 6, Write a query to filter the `sales_reporting` table to show only completed orders in the 'North' region. Then, group by `category` and show only those groups with a total quantity greater than 10.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT category, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM sales_reporting
WHERE order_status = 'Completed' AND region = 'North'
GROUP BY category
HAVING total_quantity > 10;

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Conditional Aggregation with CASE
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 6, Create a query that counts the number of completed and pending orders for each `sales_rep`. Use a CASE statement inside the COUNT function to differentiate between the two statuses.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.starterCode:
SELECT category, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM sales_reporting
WHERE order_status = 'Completed' AND region = 'North'
GROUP BY category
HAVING total_quantity > 10;
-- Project step 2: Conditional Aggregation with CASE
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query that counts the number of completed and pending orders for each `sales_rep`
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT sales_rep,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep;

main_py.content:
SELECT category, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM sales_reporting
WHERE order_status = 'Completed' AND region = 'North'
GROUP BY category
HAVING total_quantity > 10;
-- Project step 2: Conditional Aggregation with CASE
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query that counts the number of completed and pending orders for each `sales_rep`
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT sales_rep,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep;

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Label Performance with CASE
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 6, Write a query to label each `category` as 'High Performance' if the average `customer_rating` is above 4.5, otherwise label it as 'Needs Improvement'.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.starterCode:
SELECT sales_rep,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep;
-- Project step 3: Label Performance with CASE
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to label each `category` as 'High Performance' if the average `customer_rating` is above 4.5, otherwise label it as 'Needs Improvement'
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT category,
  CASE
    WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance'
    ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
  END AS performance_label
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;

main_py.content:
SELECT sales_rep,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep;
-- Project step 3: Label Performance with CASE
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to label each `category` as 'High Performance' if the average `customer_rating` is above 4.5, otherwise label it as 'Needs Improvement'
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT category,
  CASE
    WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance'
    ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
  END AS performance_label
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Calculate Total Sales with Discounts
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 6, Create a query to calculate the total sales for each `region`, considering discounts. Use the formula: `quantity * unit_price * (1 - discount_pct/100)` for each sale.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.starterCode:
SELECT category,
  CASE
    WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance'
    ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
  END AS performance_label
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;
-- Project step 4: Calculate Total Sales with Discounts
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query to calculate the total sales for each `region`, considering discounts
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT region,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price * (1 - COALESCE(discount_pct, 0)/100)) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;

main_py.content:
SELECT category,
  CASE
    WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance'
    ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
  END AS performance_label
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;
-- Project step 4: Calculate Total Sales with Discounts
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query to calculate the total sales for each `region`, considering discounts
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT region,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price * (1 - COALESCE(discount_pct, 0)/100)) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Identify Underperforming Products
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 5 of 6, Write a query to identify products with an average `customer_rating` below 4.0. List the `product_name` and the average rating.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.starterCode:
SELECT region,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price * (1 - COALESCE(discount_pct, 0)/100)) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;
-- Project step 5: Identify Underperforming Products
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to identify products with an average `customer_rating` below 4.0
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT product_name, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY product_name
HAVING avg_rating < 4.0;

main_py.content:
SELECT region,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price * (1 - COALESCE(discount_pct, 0)/100)) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;
-- Project step 5: Identify Underperforming Products
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to identify products with an average `customer_rating` below 4.0
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT product_name, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY product_name
HAVING avg_rating < 4.0;

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.title: Summarize Sales by Category and Status
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 6 of 6, Create a query to summarize total sales by `category` and `order_status`. Include only categories with total sales over $500.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.starterCode:
SELECT product_name, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY product_name
HAVING avg_rating < 4.0;
-- Project step 6: Summarize Sales by Category and Status
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query to summarize total sales by `category` and `order_status`
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
try_module_4_performance_exception_repor.solutionCode:
SELECT category, order_status,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category, order_status
HAVING total_sales > 500;

main_py.content:
SELECT product_name, AVG(customer_rating) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY product_name
HAVING avg_rating < 4.0;
-- Project step 6: Summarize Sales by Category and Status
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Create a query to summarize total sales by `category` and `order_status`
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT category, order_status,
  SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category, order_status
HAVING total_sales > 500;

module-4-performance-exception-report-sk.title: Introduction to Performance Exception Reporting
module-4-performance-exception-report-sk.bodyMarkdown: In this module, you'll learn how to create a performance exception report using SQL. This involves filtering data at both the row and group levels, applying conditional logic to aggregate functions, and labeling performance based on aggregate results. We'll use the `sales_reporting` table from the `sales_kpi` dataset to demonstrate these concepts.
